channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Tu runbook dice una cosa. Tu infra hace otra. ¿Cuándo fue la última vez que los comparaste?
item.description: Hay una conversación que ocurre en casi todos los equipos de Cloud Operations, y casi nunca ocurre en una reunión formal. Ocurre en el pasillo, en un mensaje directo, o en el hilo de Slack donde...

item.title: We are Hiring Full-Stack Developer!
item.description: We are looking for a strong Full-Stack Developer to execute product features and contribute to our core platform development. Your focus will be clean, scalable implementation and high-quality...

item.title: Djangonaut Diaries: Week 1, part 2 - Creating and debugging a Django project
item.description: Hi there! In the previous post, we cloned a fork of Django locally, and ensure it's installed and running its tests. Now we can start using this local copy of Django to do whatever changes we want...

item.title: Skill-Driven Development: Transferring your Craft to AI Agents
item.description: Every project has its own way of doing things — migration patterns, transaction handling, deployment quirks, that one PDF template workflow nobody wants to touch. I've been writing this stuff down...

item.title: OVH forgot they donated documentation hosting to Pandas

item.title: How to optimize images for websites (WebP, AVIF, responsive images)
item.description: Image optimization is still one of the easiest ways to improve website performance. Many websites still serve large JPEG or PNG files even though modern formats like WebP and AVIF can...

item.title: Solv Protocol's $2.7M ERC-3525 Reentrancy: How Semi-Fungible Tokens Created a Double-Minting Loophole
item.description: On March 5, 2026, an attacker turned 135 BRO tokens into 567 million — a 4.2-million-x inflation — by exploiting a reentrancy flaw in Solv Protocol's BitcoinReserveOffering vault. The haul: 38...

item.title: The 7 GCP Professional Cloud Architect Traps That Made Me Rethink My Entire Cloud Career
item.description: I passed the AWS Solutions Architect Professional on my first try. The Azure AZ-305 on my second. But the GCP Professional Cloud Architect? That exam humbled me in ways I wasn't prepared for. Here...

item.title: Building what NIST is asking for: an AI agent security proxy
item.description: NIST just closed a public RFI on AI agent security. The question they were asking, in five different ways: how do you constrain what an AI agent can do, and how do you prove it was constrained? We...

item.title: Writing maintainable react code at scale
item.description: Frontend applications rarely fail because React itself is difficult. They fail because the codebase slowly becomes harder to understand, extend, and debug. React gives developers enormous...

item.title: The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the ‘Deal of the Decade’
item.description: According to Index Ventures Partner Shardul Shah, cybersecurity startup Wiz sits “at the center of three tailwinds: AI, cloud, and security spend.” Those tailwinds powered what just became the...

item.title: Amazon Prime Video’s ad-free plan is getting a price hike
item.description: Amazon Prime Video is raising the price of its ad-free plan in the US and adding several new features. Starting April 10th, Prime Video's ad-free tier will cost $4.99 per month instead of $2.99,...

item.title: The Big 12 basketball tournament is ditching slippery LED courts for hardwood
item.description: They may be great for fan engagement and selling ads, but the Big 12 has decided to replace the innovative glass-covered LED floors at Kansas City's T-Mobile Center with a traditional hardwood...

item.title: The R2 is nearly here — can Rivian stick the landing?
item.description: On Thursday, Rivian revealed its most important vehicle to date: the R2, a midsize SUV that eventually will start at under $50,000. As a distinctive EV with an attractive sticker price, the R2 is...

item.title: INTERPOL Dismantles 45,000 Malicious IPs, Arrests 94 in Global Cybercrime
item.description: INTERPOL on Friday announced the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers used in connection with phishing, malware, and ransomware campaigns, as part of the agency's ongoing efforts...

item.title: The Pixel Buds Pro 2 are some of the best wireless earbuds $170 can buy
item.description: I upgraded from the Pixel A-series buds released in 2021 to the Pixel Buds Pro 2, and two things were immediately apparent: the A-series buds have no business sounding so good for their sub-$100...

item.title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
item.description: For decades, manufacturers have pursued automation to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and stabilize operations. That approach delivered meaningful gains, but it is no longer enough. Today’s...

item.title: Epic says its Fortnite V-bucks price hike is a ‘direct correlation’ to operating costs
item.description: Epic Games announced this week that Fortnite's in-game V-bucks currency would be getting more expensive with a somewhat glib statement: "The cost of running Fortnite has gone up a lot and we're...

item.title: Peacock is adding an AI Andy Cohen to narrate an endless stream of Bravo clips
item.description: When you open your Peacock mobile app this summer, you might see a portal leading to the AI likeness of TV host Andy Cohen on your homepage. In an announcement on Friday, NBCUniversal said Cohen's...

item.title: Peacock expands into AI-driven video, mobile-first live sports, and gaming
item.description: Peacock is betting on new AI-powered video experiences, vertical clips, and mobile games to help its growth.

item.title: Peacock’s NBA streams are going vertical
item.description: Peacock is rolling out a native vertical video format for live sports, allowing you to watch games without turning your phone sideways. The feature will roll out to NBA games first this spring and...

item.title: Storm-2561 Spreads Trojan VPN Clients via SEO Poisoning to Steal Credentials
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a credential theft campaign that employs fake virtual private network (VPN) clients distributed through search engine optimization (SEO) poisoning techniques....

item.title: Investigating a New Click-Fix Variant
item.description: Disclaimer: This report has been prepared by the Threat Research Center to enhance cybersecurity awareness and support the strengthening of defense capabilities. It is based on independent...

item.title: The MacBook Neo is a winner
item.description: It was a little surprising to see Apple decide to leap fully into the affordable laptop market, to try and compete with devices the company typically prefers to just look down its nose at. It was...

item.title: PSA: Don’t buy a $4,400 gray market Samsung TriFold on eBay
item.description: I had to buy Samsung's Galaxy Z TriFold from eBay for $4,399 and I'm pretty sure that's because Samsung doesn't want anyone to actually have this phone. I'm also afraid to turn it on. Not that...

item.title: Google's AI Search Results Love to Refer You Back to Google
item.description: The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.

item.title: The Download: how AI is used for military targeting, and the Pentagon’s war on Claude
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for...

item.title: China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
item.description: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.

item.title: PC makers are not ready for the MacBook Neo
item.description: The MacBook Neo is here, and it took no time at all for an executive from a major PC manufacturer to put their foot in their mouth trying to discuss this new competition from Apple's $600 laptop....

item.title: Academia and the “AI Brain Drain”
item.description: In 2025, Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta collectively spent US$380 billion on building artificial-intelligence tools. That number is expected to surge still higher this year, to $650 billion,...
